heading: Registration and regulation of security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants
label: 78o–10 Registration and regulation of security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants

children.heading: Security-based swap dealers
children.label: 1 Security-based swap dealers
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any person to act as a security-based swap dealer unless the person is registered as a security-based swap dealer with the Commission.

children.heading: Major security-based swap participants
children.label: 2 Major security-based swap participants
children.text: It shall be unlawful for any person to act as a major security-based swap participant unless the person is registered as a major security-based swap participant with the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A person shall register as a security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant by filing a registration application with the Commission.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The application shall be made in such form and manner as prescribed by the Commission, and shall contain such information, as the Commission considers necessary concerning the business in which the applicant is or will be engaged.

children.heading: Continual reporting
children.label: B Continual reporting
children.text: A person that is registered as a security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant shall continue to submit to the Commission reports that contain such information pertaining to the business of the person as the Commission may require.

children.heading: Expiration
children.label: 3 Expiration
children.text: Each registration under this section shall expire at such time as the Commission may prescribe by rule or regulation.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 4 Rules
children.text: Except as provided in subsections (d) and (e), the Commission may prescribe rules applicable to security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants, including rules that limit the activities of non-bank security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants.

children.heading: Transition
children.label: 5 Transition
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Commission shall issue rules under this section to provide for the registration of security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants.July 21, 2010

children.heading: Statutory disqualification
children.label: 6 Statutory disqualification
children.text: Except to the extent otherwise specifically provided by rule, regulation, or order of the Commission, it shall be unlawful for a security-based swap dealer or a major security-based swap participant to permit any person associated with a security-based swap dealer or a major security-based swap participant who is subject to a statutory disqualification to effect or be involved in effecting security-based swaps on behalf of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant, if the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant knew, or in the exercise of reasonable care should have known, of the statutory disqualification.

children.heading: Security-based swap dealer
children.label: 1 Security-based swap dealer
children.text: Any person that is required to be registered as a security-based swap dealer under this section shall register with the Commission, regardless of whether the person also is registered with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission as a swap dealer.

children.heading: Major security-based swap participant
children.label: 2 Major security-based swap participant
children.text: Any person that is required to be registered as a major security-based swap participant under this section shall register with the Commission, regardless of whether the person also is registered with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission as a major swap participant.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Commission shall adopt rules for persons that are registered as security-based swap dealers or major security-based swap participants under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Commission may not prescribe rules imposing prudential requirements on security-based swap dealers or major security-based swap participants for which there is a prudential regulator.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: B Applicability
children.text: Subparagraph (A) does not limit the authority of the Commission to prescribe rules as directed under this section.

children.heading: Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are banks
children.label: A Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are banks
children.text: Each registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant for which there is not a prudential regulator shall meet such minimum capital requirements and minimum initial and variation margin requirements as the prudential regulator shall by rule or regulation prescribe under paragraph (2)(A).

children.heading: Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are not banks
children.label: B Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are not banks
children.text: Each registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant for which there is not a prudential regulator shall meet such minimum capital requirements and minimum initial and variation margin requirements as the Commission shall by rule or regulation prescribe under paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are banks
children.label: A Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are banks
i
children.text: capital requirements; and

children.label: ii
children.text: both initial and variation margin requirements on all security-based swaps that are not cleared by a registered clearing agency.

children.heading: Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are not banks
children.label: B Security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants that are not banks
i
children.text: capital requirements; and

children.label: ii
children.text: both initial and variation margin requirements on all swaps that are not cleared by a registered clearing agency.

children.heading: Capital
children.label: C Capital
children.text: In setting capital requirements for a person that is designated as a security-based swap dealer or a major security-based swap participant for a single type or single class or category of security-based swap or activities, the prudential regulator and the Commission shall take into account the risks associated with other types of security-based swaps or classes of security-based swaps or categories of security-based swaps engaged in and the other activities conducted by that person that are not otherwise subject to regulation applicable to that person by virtue of the status of the person.

children.label: i
children.text: help ensure the safety and soundness of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: be appropriate for the risk associated with the non-cleared security-based swaps held as a security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant.

children.label: I
children.text: of the Commission to set financial responsibility rules for a broker or dealer registered pursuant to section 78(b) of this title (except for section 78(b)(11) thereof) in accordance with section 78(c)(3) of this title; orooo

children.label: II
children.text: of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission to set financial responsibility rules for a futures commission merchant or introducing broker registered pursuant to section 4f(a) of the Commodity Exchange Act [] (except for section 4f(a)(3) [] thereof) in accordance with section 4f(b) of the Commodity Exchange Act [].7 U.S.C. 6f(a)7 U.S.C. 6f(a)(3)7 U.S.C. 6f(b)

children.heading: Futures commission merchants and other dealers
children.label: ii Futures commission merchants and other dealers
children.text: A futures commission merchant, introducing broker, broker, or dealer shall maintain sufficient capital to comply with the stricter of any applicable capital requirements to which such futures commission merchant, introducing broker, broker, or dealer is subject to under this chapter or the Commodity Exchange Act [ et seq.].7 U.S.C. 1

children.label: i
children.text: preserving the financial integrity of markets trading security-based swaps; and

children.label: ii
children.text: preserving the stability of the United States financial system.

children.heading: Comparability of capital and margin requirements
children.label: D Comparability of capital and margin requirements

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The prudential regulators, the Commission, and the Securities and Exchange Commission shall periodically (but not less frequently than annually) consult on minimum capital requirements and minimum initial and variation margin requirements.

children.label: I
children.text: security-based swap dealers; and

children.label: II
children.text: major security-based swap participants.

children.heading: Applicability with respect to counterparties
children.label: 4 Applicability with respect to counterparties
children.text: The requirements of paragraphs (2)(A)(ii) and (2)(B)(ii) shall not apply to a security-based swap in which a counterparty qualifies for an exception under or satisfies the criteria in .section 78c–3(g)(1) of this titlesection 78c–3(g)(4) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: shall make such reports as are required by the Commission, by rule or regulation, regarding the transactions and positions and financial condition of the registered security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant;

children.label: i
children.text: for which there is a prudential regulator, shall keep books and records of all activities related to the business as a security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant in such form and manner and for such period as may be prescribed by the Commission by rule or regulation; and

children.label: ii
children.text: for which there is no prudential regulator, shall keep books and records in such form and manner and for such period as may be prescribed by the Commission by rule or regulation; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall keep books and records described in subparagraph (B) open to inspection and examination by any representative of the Commission.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 2 Rules
children.text: The Commission shall adopt rules governing reporting and recordkeeping for security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant shall maintain daily trading records of the security-based swaps of the registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant and all related records (including related cash or forward transactions) and recorded communications, including electronic mail, instant messages, and recordings of telephone calls, for such period as may be required by the Commission by rule or regulation.

children.heading: Information requirements
children.label: 2 Information requirements
children.text: The daily trading records shall include such information as the Commission shall require by rule or regulation.

children.heading: Counterparty records
children.label: 3 Counterparty records
children.text: Each registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant shall maintain daily trading records for each counterparty in a manner and form that is identifiable with each security-based swap transaction.

children.heading: Audit trail
children.label: 4 Audit trail
children.text: Each registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant shall maintain a complete audit trail for conducting comprehensive and accurate trade reconstructions.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 5 Rules
children.text: The Commission shall adopt rules governing daily trading records for security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants.

children.label: A
children.text: fraud, manipulation, and other abusive practices involving security-based swaps (including security-based swaps that are offered but not entered into);

children.label: B
children.text: diligent supervision of the business of the registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant;

children.label: C
children.text: adherence to all applicable position limits; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other matters as the Commission determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Responsibilities with respect to special entities
children.label: 2 Responsibilities with respect to special entities

children.heading: Advising special entities
children.label: A Advising special entities
children.text: A security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant that acts as an advisor to special entity regarding a security-based swap shall comply with the requirements of paragraph (4) with respect to such special entity.1So in original. Probably should be followed by “a”.1

children.heading: Entering of security-based swaps with respect to special entities
children.label: B Entering of security-based swaps with respect to special entities
children.text: A security-based swap dealer that enters into or offers to enter into security-based swap with a special entity shall comply with the requirements of paragraph (5) with respect to such special entity.1

children.label: i
children.text: a Federal agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: a State, State agency, city, county, municipality, or other political subdivision of a State or;

children.label: iii
children.text: any employee benefit plan, as defined in section 3 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 ();29 U.S.C. 1002

children.label: iv
children.text: any governmental plan, as defined in section 3 of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (); or29 U.S.C. 1002

children.label: v
children.text: any endowment, including an endowment that is an organization described in .section 501(c)(3) of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: establish a duty for a security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant to verify that any counterparty meets the eligibility standards for an eligible contract participant;

children.label: i
children.text: information about the material risks and characteristics of the security-based swap;

children.label: ii
children.text: any material incentives or conflicts of interest that the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant may have in connection with the security-based swap; and

children.label: I
children.text: for cleared security-based swaps, upon the request of the counterparty, receipt of the daily mark of the transaction from the appropriate derivatives clearing organization; and

children.label: II
children.text: for uncleared security-based swaps, receipt of the daily mark of the transaction from the security-based swap dealer or the major security-based swap participant;

children.label: C
children.text: establish a duty for a security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant to communicate in a fair and balanced manner based on principles of fair dealing and good faith; and

children.label: D
children.text: establish such other standards and requirements as the Commission may determine are appropriate in the public interest, for the protection of investors, or otherwise in furtherance of the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Special requirements for security-based swap dealers acting as advisors
children.label: 4 Special requirements for security-based swap dealers acting as advisors
i
children.text: to employ any device, scheme, or artifice to defraud any special entity or prospective customer who is a special entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: to engage in any transaction, practice, or course of business that operates as a fraud or deceit on any special entity or prospective customer who is a special entity; or

children.label: iii
children.text: to engage in any act, practice, or course of business that is fraudulent, deceptive, or manipulative.

children.heading: Duty
children.label: B Duty
children.text: Any security-based swap dealer that acts as an advisor to a special entity shall have a duty to act in the best interests of the special entity.

children.label: i
children.text: the financial status of the special entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: the tax status of the special entity;

children.label: iii
children.text: the investment or financing objectives of the special entity; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information that the Commission may prescribe by rule or regulation.

children.heading: Special requirements for security-based swap dealers as counterparties to special entities
children.label: 5 Special requirements for security-based swap dealers as counterparties to special entities
I
children.text: has sufficient knowledge to evaluate the transaction and risks;

children.label: II
children.text: is not subject to a statutory disqualification;

children.label: III
children.text: is independent of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant;

children.label: IV
children.text: undertakes a duty to act in the best interests of the counterparty it represents;

children.label: V
children.text: makes appropriate disclosures;

children.label: VI
children.text: will provide written representations to the special entity regarding fair pricing and the appropriateness of the transaction; and

children.label: VII
children.text: in the case of employee benefit plans subject to the Employee Retirement Income Security act of 1974 [ et seq.], is a fiduciary as defined in section 3 of that Act (); and229 U.S.C. 100129 U.S.C. 1002So in original. Probably should be capitalized.2

children.label: ii
children.text: before the initiation of the transaction, disclose to the special entity in writing the capacity in which the security-based swap dealer is acting.

children.heading: Commission authority
children.label: B Commission authority
children.text: The Commission may establish such other standards and requirements under this paragraph as the Commission may determine are appropriate in the public interest, for the protection of investors, or otherwise in furtherance of the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 6 Rules
children.text: The Commission shall prescribe rules under this subsection governing business conduct standards for security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants.

children.label: A
children.text: initiated by a special entity on an exchange or security-based swaps execution facility; and

children.label: B
children.text: the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant does not know the identity of the counterparty to the transaction.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each registered security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant shall conform with such standards as may be prescribed by the Commission, by rule or regulation, that relate to timely and accurate confirmation, processing, netting, documentation, and valuation of all security-based swaps.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 2 Rules
children.text: The Commission shall adopt rules governing documentation standards for security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants.

children.heading: Monitoring of trading
children.label: 1 Monitoring of trading
children.text: The security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant shall monitor its trading in security-based swaps to prevent violations of applicable position limits.

children.heading: Risk management procedures
children.label: 2 Risk management procedures
children.text: The security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant shall establish robust and professional risk management systems adequate for managing the day-to-day business of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant.

children.label: A
children.text: terms and conditions of its security-based swaps;

children.label: B
children.text: security-based swap trading operations, mechanisms, and practices;

children.label: C
children.text: financial integrity protections relating to security-based swaps; and

children.label: D
children.text: other information relevant to its trading in security-based swaps.

children.label: A
children.text: establish and enforce internal systems and procedures to obtain any necessary information to perform any of the functions described in this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide the information to the Commission and to the prudential regulator for the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant, as applicable, on request.

children.label: A
children.text: establish structural and institutional safeguards to ensure that the activities of any person within the firm relating to research or analysis of the price or market for any security-based swap or acting in a role of providing clearing activities or making determinations as to accepting clearing customers are separated by appropriate informational partitions within the firm from the review, pressure, or oversight of persons whose involvement in pricing, trading, or clearing activities might potentially bias their judgment or supervision and contravene the core principles of open access and the business conduct standards described in this chapter; and

children.label: B
children.text: address such other issues as the Commission determines to be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: adopt any process or take any action that results in any unreasonable restraint of trade; or

children.label: B
children.text: impose any material anticompetitive burden on trading or clearing.

children.heading: Rules
children.label: 7 Rules
children.text: The Commission shall prescribe rules under this subsection governing duties of security-based swap dealers and major security-based swap participants.

children.heading: Designation of chief compliance officer
children.label: k Designation of chief compliance officer

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant shall designate an individual to serve as a chief compliance officer.

children.label: A
children.text: report directly to the board or to the senior officer of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant;

children.label: B
children.text: review the compliance of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant with respect to the security-based swap dealer and major security-based swap participant requirements described in this section;

children.label: C
children.text: in consultation with the board of directors, a body performing a function similar to the board, or the senior officer of the organization, resolve any conflicts of interest that may arise;

children.label: D
children.text: be responsible for administering each policy and procedure that is required to be established pursuant to this section;

children.label: E
children.text: ensure compliance with this chapter (including regulations) relating to security-based swaps, including each rule prescribed by the Commission under this section;

children.label: i
children.text: compliance office review;

children.label: ii
children.text: look-back;

children.label: iii
children.text: internal or external audit finding;

children.label: iv
children.text: self-reported error; or

children.label: v
children.text: validated complaint; and

children.label: G
children.text: establish and follow appropriate procedures for the handling, management response, remediation, retesting, and closing of noncompliance issues.

children.label: i
children.text: the compliance of the security-based swap dealer or major swap participant with respect to this chapter (including regulations); and

children.label: ii
children.text: each policy and procedure of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant of the chief compliance officer (including the code of ethics and conflict of interest policies).

children.label: i
children.text: accompany each appropriate financial report of the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant that is required to be furnished to the Commission pursuant to this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include a certification that, under penalty of law, the compliance report is accurate and complete.

children.heading: Enforcement and administrative proceeding authority
children.label: l Enforcement and administrative proceeding authority

children.heading: Securities and Exchange Commission
children.label: A Securities and Exchange Commission
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), (C), or (D), the Commission shall have primary authority to enforce subtitle B, and the amendments made by subtitle B of the Wall Street Transparency and Accountability Act of 2010, with respect to any person.

children.heading: Prudential regulators
children.label: B Prudential regulators
children.text: The prudential regulators shall have exclusive authority to enforce the provisions of subsection (e) and other prudential requirements of this chapter (including risk management standards), with respect to security-based swap dealers or major security-based swap participants for which they are the prudential regulator.

children.heading: Violations of nonprudential requirements
children.label: i Violations of nonprudential requirements
children.text: If the appropriate Federal banking agency for security-based swap dealers or major security-based swap participants that are depository institutions has cause to believe that such security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant may have engaged in conduct that constitutes a violation of the nonprudential requirements of this section or rules adopted by the Commission thereunder, the agency may recommend in writing to the Commission that the Commission initiate an enforcement proceeding as authorized under this chapter. The recommendation shall be accompanied by a written explanation of the concerns giving rise to the recommendation.

children.heading: Violations of prudential requirements
children.label: ii Violations of prudential requirements
children.text: If the Commission has cause to believe that a securities-based swap dealer or major securities-based swap participant that has a prudential regulator may have engaged in conduct that constitute a violation of the prudential requirements of subsection (e) or rules adopted thereunder, the Commission may recommend in writing to the prudential regulator that the prudential regulator initiate an enforcement proceeding as authorized under this chapter. The recommendation shall be accompanied by a written explanation of the concerns giving rise to the recommendation.3So in original. Probably should be “constitutes”.3

children.heading: Initiation of enforcement proceeding by prudential regulator
children.label: i Initiation of enforcement proceeding by prudential regulator
children.text: If the Commission does not initiate an enforcement proceeding before the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the Commission receives a written report under subsection (C)(i), the prudential regulator may initiate an enforcement proceeding.4So in original. Probably should be “subparagraph”.4

children.heading: Initiation of enforcement proceeding by Commission
children.label: ii Initiation of enforcement proceeding by Commission
children.text: If the prudential regulator does not initiate an enforcement proceeding before the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date on which the prudential regulator receives a written report under subsection (C)(ii), the Commission may initiate an enforcement proceeding.4

children.heading: Censure, denial, suspension; notice and hearing
children.label: 2 Censure, denial, suspension; notice and hearing
A
children.text: has committed or omitted any act, or is subject to an order or finding, enumerated in subparagraph (A), (D), or (E) of paragraph (4) of section 78(b) of this title;o

children.label: B
children.text: has been convicted of any offense specified in subparagraph (B) of such paragraph (4) within 10 years of the commencement of the proceedings under this subsection;

children.label: C
children.text: is enjoined from any action, conduct, or practice specified in subparagraph (C) of such paragraph (4);

children.label: D
children.text: is subject to an order or a final order specified in subparagraph (F) or (H), respectively, of such paragraph (4); or

children.label: E
children.text: has been found by a foreign financial regulatory authority to have committed or omitted any act, or violated any foreign statute or regulation, enumerated in subparagraph (G) of such paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: has committed or omitted any act, or is subject to an order or finding, enumerated in subparagraph (A), (D), or (E) of paragraph (4) of section 78(b) of this title;o

children.label: B
children.text: has been convicted of any offense specified in subparagraph (B) of such paragraph (4) within 10 years of the commencement of the proceedings under this subsection;

children.label: C
children.text: is enjoined from any action, conduct, or practice specified in subparagraph (C) of such paragraph (4);

children.label: D
children.text: is subject to an order or a final order specified in subparagraph (F) or (H), respectively, of such paragraph (4); or

children.label: E
children.text: has been found by a foreign financial regulatory authority to have committed or omitted any act, or violated any foreign statute or regulation, enumerated in subparagraph (G) of such paragraph (4).

children.label: A
children.text: for any person as to whom an order under paragraph (3) is in effect, without the consent of the Commission, willfully to become, or to be, associated with a security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant in contravention of such order; or

children.label: B
children.text: for any security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant to permit such a person, without the consent of the Commission, to become or remain a person associated with the security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant in contravention of such order, if such security-based swap dealer or major security-based swap participant knew, or in the exercise of reasonable care should have known, of such order.
